summary: A crisp sound‑board remix captures a magical early‑'72 show with explosive opener and standout jams on Looks Like Rain, Truckin', and The Other One.
review: The Miller SBD remix delivers clear, balanced audio that puts Bob Weir front‑and‑center and gives Jerry’s guitar a sweet, steel‑like tone on Looks Like Rain.  Musically the concert is praised as one of the strongest of the year – an explosive Greatest Story opener, a varied first set and a second‑set jam marathon highlighted by Truckin' → The Other One → Me & My Uncle.  A few minor glitches (a splice and a missing intro) keep the recording from being perfect, but overall it’s a must‑hear performance.

ai_rating.rationale: The majority of 12 reviews are 5‑star, praising both the show and the remix; only a few note minor flaws, yielding an overall high rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, well‑balanced mix with Bob Weir up front and Jerry’s tone clear; a splice/hiccup is noted by one reviewer and the first few seconds of Greatest Story are missing in the SBD, but overall clarity is excellent.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Explosive Greatest Story opener, a varied first set with strong renditions of Cold Rain & Snow and Chinatown Shuffle, followed by an extended Truckin' jam that segues into The Other One and Me & My Uncle – a second‑set described as magical.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Pedal‑steel‑like tone on Looks Like Rain; mix keeps him balanced rather than overly forward.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass harmonies shine on Looks Like Rain; praised for solid groove and tempo throughout the show.
band_member_comments.Bob: Weir’s rhythm guitar and vocals are mixed up front, giving the set a strong vocal lead.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith is introduced as the new piano player; keyboards add depth but receive no specific criticism or praise beyond that.
band_member_comments.Drums: All players praised; one note of single‑drummer feel on Not Fade Away.
